message:
feat(wish): add shell-aware exec command parser with quote and escape handling

Replace naive whitespace splitting of SSH exec commands with a proper
argv-style parser that preserves quoted segments and backslash escapes.
The previous `split_whitespace` approach broke commands containing
arguments with spaces (e.g. `cmd --path "/tmp/foo bar"`), silently
splitting them into separate tokens.

- Add `parse_exec_command_args()` supporting single/double quotes and
  backslash escaping outside single quotes
- Return `None` on unmatched quotes; caller falls back to whitespace
  split with a warning log
- Add unit tests covering basic tokenization, quoted strings, escape
  sequences, unterminated quotes, and empty quoted arguments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
